summary: Two functions can be combined into a new one. You can add, subtract, multiply or divide them point by point, or compose them — feeding the output of one into the other. Composition f(g(x)) is usually not the same as g(f(x)), so order matters.
method: For a sum, difference, product or quotient, just apply the operation to f(x) and g(x).
For a composition f ∘ g, replace every variable in f by the whole expression g(x).
Remember the domain: a composition only accepts inputs that g accepts and that land in the domain of f.

example.title: Compose two functions
steps: g(f(x)) = x² + 1  — substitute f into g.
They are different, so order matters.
